title: Plastics process operatives
description: Laminate layers of fiberglass on molds to form boat decks and hulls, bodies for golf carts, automobiles, or other products. Set up, operate, or tend machines to extrude or draw thermoplastic or metal materials into tubes, rods, hoses, wire, bars, or structural shapes. Set up, operate, or tend forging machines to taper, shape, or form metal or plastic parts. (Based on 13 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Inspect, clean, and assemble molds before beginning work.
Pat or press layers of saturated mat or cloth into place on molds, and smooth out wrinkles and air bubbles.
Apply lacquers and waxes to mold surfaces, and mask off areas not to be laminated using appropriate materials.
Apply layers of plastic resin to mold surfaces before placement of fiberglass mats, repeating until desired thickness is reached.
Cure materials by letting them set at room temperature or placing them under heat lamps, or baking them in ovens.
Repair or modify damaged glass-fiber parts, ensuring they meet specifications for thickness and contours after repair.
Trim excess materials from molds using hand shears or trimming knives, and check completed products for conformance to specifications.
Bond wood reinforcing strips to decks and cabin structures of watercraft using resin-saturated fiberglass.
Mix catalysts into resins, saturate cloth and mats with the mixture, and select precut fiberglass mats and wood-bracing materials.
Spray chopped fiberglass, resins, and catalysts onto prepared molds using pneumatic spray guns, and smooth seams and release air bubbles.
Smooth and clean inner surfaces of molds, fill imperfections, and perform maintenance work such as cleaning and oiling machines.
Observe continuous operation of automatic machines to ensure product specifications are met and detect malfunctions, making adjustments as necessary.
Measure and visually inspect products for defects, and read specifications or work orders to plan production sequences.
Set up, operate, or tend machines to mold, cast, or forge plastic or metal parts, adjusting controls as necessary.
Remove finished or cured products from molds using appropriate equipment and clean work area regularly.
Maintain inventories of materials and monitor the level of fluids in machines, replenishing as necessary.
Observe gauges to verify machine operation, and record production output and maintain records.
Train new workers in machine operation and perform basic assembly tasks using power and hand tools.
Install tools, fixtures, and attachments on machines and adjust settings to ensure proper operation.
Troubleshoot machine operations and perform minor equipment maintenance or repairs using hand tools.
Move toolholders manually or by turning handwheels, or engage automatic feeding mechanisms to feed tools to workpieces.
Turn valve handles to direct the flow of coolant onto work areas or to coat disks with spinning compounds, and fill oil cups to control metal coolant flow.
Sharpen cutting tools and drill bits using bench grinders and other equipment as needed.
Use equipment designed to join sheet metal, position guides, secure workpieces against fixtures or stops, and set up machines accordingly.
Set up, operate, or tend machines to saw, cut, shear, or perform other operations on metal or plastic material.
Read work orders, blueprints, or production schedules to determine specifications, including dimensions and tolerances.
Install, adjust, and remove dies, machine screws, and other tooling on machines that extrude thermoplastic or metal materials.
Measure and inspect workpieces and extruded products to ensure conformance to specifications and locate defects.
Start machines, monitor their operations, and make adjustments to ensure products conform to specifications while troubleshooting as needed.
Clean and lubricate machines, maintain work areas, and replace worn or defective components as necessary.
Load workpieces or materials into machines and weigh or mix thermoplastic materials and coloring pigments for production.
Confer with other workers about machine setups, operational specifications, and direct or train them on procedures as required.
Adjust machine controls and settings, including pressures, speeds, and depths, to regulate operations according to product specifications.
Examine completed workpieces for defects and sort them according to types of flaws.
Direct and signal other workers in changing rolls, operating equipment, and managing production flow.
